overview: Azorius Control in Modern relies on surviving early aggression and combo attempts through disruptive board wipes like Supreme Verdict. Once the board is clear, the deck transitions into a value grind using Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard to generate card advantage. By outlasting opponents through efficient resource management and graveyard synergy, the deck secures victory in the late game.
win_conditions: Outvaluing opponents with Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard
Resetting the board with Supreme Verdict to regain initiative
Stabilizing against aggressive strategies and closing out with late-game creatures

keep_criteria: Hands with two to three lands and early interaction
Supreme Verdict on turn three or four
Solitude or Kaheera, the Orphanguard with sufficient mana sources
mulligan_criteria: Five or more cards with no interaction
Hands lacking Supreme Verdict or value engines against aggressive decks

early_game: Prioritize land drops and hold up mana for Supreme Verdict
Disrupt opponent's early board state before committing resources
Use Kaheera, the Orphanguard to dig for answers if the graveyard is populated
mid_game: Deploy Solitude to establish a durable blocker and value source
Time Supreme Verdict to clear multiple threats simultaneously
Cycle through the graveyard using Kaheera and Solitude to find late-game answers
late_game: Leverage Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard to generate massive card advantage
Maintain board presence while digging for win conditions or additional control
Outpace opponent's resources through continuous value generation

key_cards.name: Supreme Verdict
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to clear aggressive starts or protect value engines; use sparingly to maintain resource parity

key_cards.name: Kaheera, the Orphanguard
key_cards.notes: Activate ability to draw cards when the graveyard is full; pair with graveyard-filling strategies to sustain the grind

key_cards.name: Solitude
key_cards.notes: Use exile ability to find answers or win conditions; keep on board to outvalue opponents through repeated activations | Used to deal with Nadu while it is on the stack, preventing the opponent from drawing a card instead of answering it after it resolves. (from gameplay)

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for Supreme Verdict before making irreversible plays
Activate Kaheera, the Orphanguard and Solitude to find answers before attacking
Prioritize clearing threats that cannot be answered by value engines
Manage graveyard state to maximize Kaheera and Solitude activations
Prioritize casting Solitude on Nadu while it is on the stack rather than waiting for it to resolve, to prevent the opponent from drawing a card.

matchups.advice: Play Supreme Verdict on curve to reset the board, then stabilize with Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard to outvalue the opponent.
Disrupt key pieces with Supreme Verdict, then use Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard to dig for answers and outlast the combo attempt.
Trade efficiently in the early game, use Supreme Verdict to reset board states, and transition into a value grind with Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard.
Race the opponent's value engines by aggressively deploying Solitude and Kaheera, the Orphanguard, while timing Supreme Verdict to clear their board.

Use Dress Down to temporarily remove Nadu's abilities for a turn. (from gameplay) Be aware that Sun Cleanser removes all energy counters and prevents gaining new ones, completely shutting down your energy-based sweepers. (from gameplay) Watch out for Vexing Devil, which stops counter magic and instant-speed interaction. (from gameplay)
